source.title: A broadly neutralizing humanized ACE2-targeting antibody against SARS-CoV-2 variants

initial_request.research_problem: The rapid emergence of SARS-CoV-2 variants with RBD mutations that escape existing antibody therapies necessitates broadly effective countermeasures.
initial_request.known_context: SARS-CoV-2 variants with RBD mutations (e.g., D614G, B.1.1.7, B.1.351) are spreading and can escape existing antibody therapies. ACE2 is the conserved host receptor for SARSr-CoVs.
constraints: Must not downregulate ACE2 or impair its enzymatic activity
Must be safe in non-human primates
input_data: SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2 spike pseudotyped viruses
quantifiable_goals: High binding affinity to hACE2 (KD < 10 nM)
In vivo efficacy in mouse model

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SARS-CoV-2 variants with RBD mutations are emerging and escaping existing antibody therapies.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Existing antibody therapies target the RBD, which is prone to mutation. A host-directed approach may provide broader neutralization.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Targeting the conserved host receptor ACE2 with a blocking antibody may provide broad neutralization against SARSr-CoVs and variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Pursue isolation of ACE2-blocking monoclonal antibodies.
evolution_trajectory.observation: ACE2 is the conserved receptor for SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2; RBD mutations can escape RBD-targeting antibodies.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Hypothesis supported by known biology; proceed to antibody generation.

evidence.quote_or_span: The successive emergences and accelerating spread of novel severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) lineages and evolved resistance to some ongoing clinical therapeutics increase the risks associated with the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic.
An urgent intervention for broadly effective therapies to limit the morbidity and mortality of COVID-19 and future transmission events from SARS-related coronaviruses (SARSr-CoVs) is needed.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that ACE2-blocking antibody can provide broad neutralization.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: No ACE2-blocking antibody with broad activity has been isolated.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Immunizing mice with hACE2 will elicit antibodies that block ACE2-RBD interaction.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Immunize BALB/c mice with hACE2, generate hybridomas, and screen for ACE2-blocking antibodies.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Five clones of MAbs blocked infection with SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2 pseudotyped virus.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Lead clone h11B11 identified for further characterization.

evidence.quote_or_span: To generate murine anti-hACE2 antibodies, BALB/c mice received hACE2 (19–615) soluble antigens in a prime-boost immunization regimen with a 4-week interval.
After screening hybridoma supernatants, five clones of the MAbs that blocked HEK293T-hACE2 cell infection with SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2 spike pseudotyped virus were identified

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which clone has the best inhibitory activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Clone h11B11 will show significant inhibitory activity against pseudovirus infection.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test inhibitory activity of clone h11B11 against SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2 pseudoviruses.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Clone h11B11 exhibited significant inhibitory activity against pseudotyped virus infection.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: h11B11 selected for humanization to reduce immunogenicity.

evidence.quote_or_span: clone h11B11 exhibited significant inhibitory activity against pseudotyped virus infection

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Murine antibody h11B11 shows inhibitory activity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Murine antibodies can be immunogenic in humans.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Humanizing h11B11 will retain binding while reducing immunogenicity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate that humanization did not impair binding.

evidence.quote_or_span: Here, we isolate and humanize an angiotensin-converting enzyme-2 (ACE2)-blocking monoclonal antibody (MAb), named h11B11, which exhibits potent inhibitory activity against SARS-CoV and circulating global SARS-CoV-2 lineages.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Humanized h11B11 retains binding kinetics comparable to parental murine antibody.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare binding kinetics of humanized and parental h11B11 to hACE2 by SPR.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Binding kinetics of humanized and parental MAbs to hACE2 was consistent.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to functional characterization of humanized h11B11.

evidence.quote_or_span: Surface plasmon resonance (SPR) showed that the binding kinetics of humanized and parental MAbs to hACE2 was consistent

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Humanized h11B11 validated to retain binding.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does h11B11 block RBD binding, and does it affect ACE2 surface expression or enzymatic activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: h11B11 blocks RBD-ACE2 interaction and does not downregulate ACE2 or impair its enzymatic activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess RBD blocking, ACE2 surface levels, and enzymatic activity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: h11B11 blocked RBD binding; membrane ACE2 levels unaffected; enzymatic activity not impaired.

evidence.quote_or_span: the levels of membrane-expressed hACE2 were not affected after incubating with h11B11
Then, 25 $\mu$ l h11B11 proteins at various final concentrations of 100, 200, and 400 $\mu$ g/ml or hACE2 inhibitor (MLN-4760, Sigma, 5.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: h11B11 blocks RBD binding and preserves ACE2 function.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does h11B11 neutralize diverse SARS-CoV-2 variants and other SARSr-CoVs?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: h11B11 will broadly neutralize SARS-CoV, SARS-CoV-2, and variants but not NL63 due to different RBD binding mode.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test neutralization against 34 pseudoviruses including variants D614G, B.1.1.7, B.1.351, and NL63.
evolution_trajectory.observation: h11B11 neutralized SARS-CoV, SARS-CoV-2, and variants; no activity against NL63; S19P ACE2 variant completely resistant.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Structural analysis needed to understand mechanism and NL63 escape.

evidence.quote_or_span: Next, we set out to investigate the inhibitory activity of h11B11 against 34 pseudoviruses, including high-frequency SARS-CoV-2 variants and combined variants with D614G, across the entire S protein.
However, the S19P variant exhibited complete resistance to the MAb, revealing the importance of S19 on NTH for h11B11 binding to hACE2.
59 $\mu$ g/ml) in all three pseudoviruses expressing the SARS-CoV-2 S antigens of lineages with sustained transmission (D614G, B.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: h11B11 broadly neutralizes SARS-CoV-2 variants but not NL63; S19P ACE2 variant resistant.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What is the structural basis for broad neutralization and NL63 escape?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: h11B11 binds N-terminal helix of ACE2, sterically clashing with SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2 RBDs but not NL63 RBD.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Determine cryo-EM and crystal structures of h11B11-ACE2 complex and compare with RBD-ACE2 complexes.
evolution_trajectory.observation: h11B11 binds N-terminal helix of ACE2; steric clash with SARS-CoV and SARS-CoV-2 RBDs; no clash with NL63 RBD.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Structural insights support in vivo efficacy testing.

evidence.quote_or_span: The structure of the h11B11/hACE2 complex was determined at a resolution of 3.8 Å
h11b11 blocks SARS-CoV-RBD and SARS-CoV-2-RBD to bind to hACE2 mainly through N-terminal epitope competition and steric clash
No structural evidence directly indicates that the MAb h11B11 inhibits the binding of HCoV-NL63-RBD and ACE2
Fig. 5 Structural basis of the competitive binding of h11B11 and RBDs with hACE2.

The binding profiles of h11B11 to five hACE2 variants (S19P, I21T, K26R, N33D, and D38E) in the N-terminal helix (NTH), which may greatly affect the stability of receptors and MAb binding, were further analyzed.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does h11B11 protect against SARS-CoV-2 in vivo?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Prophylactic and therapeutic administration of h11B11 will reduce viral load in hACE2 transgenic mice.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test h11B11 in hACE2 transgenic mice with prophylactic (pre-exposure) and therapeutic (post-exposure) dosing.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Prophylactic groups: viral titers below detection limit in 9/10 mice; therapeutic groups: ~10-fold reduction in viral load.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Promising efficacy warrants safety evaluation in non-human primates.

evidence.quote_or_span: for the prophylactic (pre-exposure) groups, both high- and low-dose administration of h11B11 resulted in viral titers in the lungs below the detection limit for nine of ten mice
Animals in the pre-exposure groups were injected with 5 or 25 mg/kg antibody one day before the viral challenge.
In the post-exposure groups, the mice were administered with 5 or 25 mg/kg antibody one day after the viral challenge.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: h11B11 shows in vivo efficacy in mice.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is h11B11 safe in a relevant animal model?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Repeated high-dose h11B11 infusion in cynomolgus monkeys will not cause significant changes in blood pressure, hematology, or clinical chemistry.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Administer h11B11 to cynomolgus monkeys at 60 and 180 mg/kg once weekly for 3 weeks and monitor safety parameters.
evolution_trajectory.observation: No significant changes in blood pressure, hematology, or clinical chemistry; minor blood pressure fluctuations within normal range.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Safety profile supports further development, but limitations noted.

evidence.quote_or_span: An SPR assay confirmed that this MAb shows analogous binding affinities to hACE2 and cynomolgus ACE2
Compared with the predose measurements, the mean diastolic and systolic blood pressure of all animals in the following 6 days after the second dosing showed only minor changes, from 90 to 102 mm Hg
These indexes of the animals in the two groups were not significantly affected by multiple administrations
injections of three doses of 180 mg/kg h11B11 did not induce drastic blood pressure changes or obvious toxicological damage in nonhuman primate models.
Cynomolgus monkeys were administered via repeated intravenous infusion (60 or 180 mg/kg at once a week for 3 weeks).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: h11B11 shows efficacy and safety in animal models.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the limitations of h11B11?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: S19P ACE2 polymorphism and NL63 inactivity are key limitations; comprehensive GLP safety study needed.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations: S19P resistance, no NL63 activity, need for comprehensive safety evaluation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: S19P variant completely resistant; no structural evidence for NL63 inhibition; comprehensive GLP-compliant safety evaluation needed.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations inform final validation and future work.

evidence.quote_or_span: However, the S19P variant exhibited complete resistance to the MAb, revealing the importance of S19 on NTH for h11B11 binding to hACE2.
No structural evidence directly indicates that the MAb h11B11 inhibits the binding of HCoV-NL63-RBD and ACE2
Considering the limited animal numbers and the lack of histopathology assays, a comprehensive GLP-compliant safety evaluation is needed.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is h11B11 a promising therapeutic candidate overall?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: h11B11 is a broadly promising therapeutic candidate against SARSr-CoVs and variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude that ACE2-blocking MAb h11B11 represents a promising candidate with superior affinity and safety compared to soluble ACE2 decoys.
evolution_trajectory.observation: h11B11 shows broad neutralization, high affinity, in vivo efficacy, and safety; limitations include S19P resistance and NL63 inactivity.

evidence.quote_or_span: our work shows that the ACE2-blocking MAb represents a broadly promising therapeutic candidate against emergence, re-emergence, and future zoonotic transmission events from SARSr-CoVs and variants.

The binding profiles of h11B11 to five hACE2 variants (S19P, I21T, K26R, N33D, and D38E) in the N-terminal helix (NTH), which may greatly affect the stability of receptors and MAb binding, were further analyzed.
However, the S19P variant exhibited complete resistance to the MAb, revealing the importance of S19 on NTH for h11B11 binding to hACE2.
Meanwhile, we speculate two reasons for the S19P variant eliminating the interaction between hACE2 and MAb h11B11.

The coding sequences of SARS-CoV-RBD (residues 306–527, accession number: NC\_004718), SARS-CoV-2-RBD (residues 319–541, accession number EPI\_ISL\_402119), hACE2 (residues 19–615, accession number BAJ21180), and hACE2 variants (S19P, I21T, K26R, N33D, and D38E) fused with N-terminal native signal peptides and C-terminal 6× His tag were, respectively, cloned into the pCAGGS expression vector (Addgene) using the EcoRI and XhoI restriction sites.
We found that a low-frequency locus, S19P (rs73635825, $p = 7.

success_verification.final_conclusion: h11B11 is a broadly promising therapeutic candidate against SARSr-CoVs and variants, with superior affinity and safety profile compared to soluble ACE2 decoys.
limitations: S19P ACE2 polymorphism completely abolishes binding
Limited animal numbers and lack of histopathology in safety study
